topic: refine and upgrade the sk-code workflow sub-skills (code-implement, code-quality, code-debug, code-verify): assess real usefulness, coverage gaps, cross-skill overlap, and friction across each SKILL.md, references, and assets; produce ranked evidence-grounded upgrade proposals

focus: Inventory and boundary analysis of the four sk-code workflow sub-skills
noveltyJustification: 5 of 6 findings were fully new and 1 was partially new as initial inventory synthesis, with no simplicity bonus beyond boundary clarification.
keyQuestions: What is each workflow sub-skill genuinely useful for in current form?
Where do code-implement, code-quality, code-debug, and code-verify overlap or contradict each other?
What coverage gaps exist across their SKILL.md, references, and assets?
What friction do users or agents encounter when routing, loading, applying, or verifying these skills?
Which upgrade proposals have the highest leverage and clearest evidence?
answeredQuestions: Initial usefulness and lifecycle boundaries are mapped.
Initial cross-skill friction candidates are identified.
ruledOut: Full asset-level inspection in iteration 1
Treating stale path wording as complete missing dependency

focus: Asset-level evidence for code-implement and code-quality against the cross-mode lifecycle
noveltyJustification: 5 of 6 findings were fully new and 1 was partially new from deeper asset/script inspection; no simplicity bonus was applied because a routing contradiction remains unresolved.
keyQuestions: What is each workflow sub-skill genuinely useful for in current form?
Where do code-implement, code-quality, code-debug, and code-verify overlap or contradict each other?
What coverage gaps exist across their SKILL.md, references, and assets?
What friction do users or agents encounter when routing, loading, applying, or verifying these skills?
Which upgrade proposals have the highest leverage and clearest evidence?
answeredQuestions: code-implement usefulness and delegated-resource friction are evidenced.
code-quality usefulness, local assets, stale path vocabulary, and review-vs-quality asset conflict are evidenced.
ruledOut: Full code-debug/code-verify inspection in iteration 2
Adding local references to code-implement as the primary fix
Moving OpenCode checklists into code-quality without further design review

focus: Deeper per-skill evidence for code-debug and code-verify, including assets, scripts, and handoff friction
noveltyJustification: 5 of 6 findings were fully new and 1 was partially new from debug/verify asset, script, and handoff inspection; no simplicity bonus was applied because path-vocabulary contradictions remain unresolved.
keyQuestions: What is each workflow sub-skill genuinely useful for in current form?
Where do code-implement, code-quality, code-debug, and code-verify overlap or contradict each other?
What coverage gaps exist across their SKILL.md, references, and assets?
What friction do users or agents encounter when routing, loading, applying, or verifying these skills?
Which upgrade proposals have the highest leverage and clearest evidence?
answeredQuestions: code-debug usefulness, local checklist coverage, delegated-resource friction, and lifecycle handoff are evidenced.
code-verify usefulness, local assets/scripts, non-mutating boundary, and concrete stack-folder verifier defect are evidenced.
ruledOut: Treating missing local Webflow debug/verify assets as a hard dependency
Treating verify_stack_folders.py as currently healthy
Re-inspecting code-implement and code-quality deeply in iteration 3

focus: Cross-skill overlap and lifecycle seams across code-implement -> code-quality -> code-debug -> code-verify plus parent sk-code routing files
noveltyJustification: 3 of 5 findings were fully new and 2 were partially new from cross-mode synthesis of parent routing, registry tool surfaces, shared lifecycle transitions, and sibling handoff contracts.
keyQuestions: Where do code-implement, code-quality, code-debug, and code-verify overlap or contradict each other?
What friction do users or agents encounter when routing, loading, applying, or verifying these skills?
Which upgrade proposals have the highest leverage and clearest evidence?
answeredQuestions: Cross-mode overlap is mostly intentional lifecycle overlap, with stale path vocabulary and missing payload schema as the main seams.
Parent routing should remain routing-only; lifecycle handoff metadata belongs in shared references or packet contracts, not hub process logic.
ruledOut: Editing the hub router to encode lifecycle sequencing directly
Treating checklist overlap between code-implement and code-quality as a duplication bug
Treating code-verify failures as an opportunity for in-place repair

focus: Ranked evidence-grounded upgrade proposal matrix for the four sk-code workflow sub-skills
noveltyJustification: 1 of 5 findings was fully new, 4 were partially new synthesis/ranking, plus a 0.10 simplicity bonus for reducing open upgrade options into a prioritized proposal model.
keyQuestions: Which upgrade proposals have the highest leverage and clearest evidence?
What friction do users or agents encounter when routing, loading, applying, or verifying these skills?
Where do code-implement, code-quality, code-debug, and code-verify overlap or contradict each other?
answeredQuestions: The top upgrade is to repair or retire/re-scope code-verify/assets/scripts/verify_stack_folders.py for the current registry and sibling surface-packet architecture.
The next-ranked upgrades are a shared handoff schema, stale path normalization, parent-vs-packet permission precedence, and explicit checklist-overlap semantics.
ruledOut: Moving lifecycle logic into the parent hub or hub-router.json
Adding duplicate local Webflow/OpenCode evidence directories under every workflow packet
Treating code-verify as responsible for in-session repair of the broken verifier

focus: README and checklist contradiction pass against ranked sk-code workflow sub-skill proposals
noveltyJustification: 1 of 5 findings was fully new and 3 were partially new corroborations/broadenings, with no simplicity bonus because convergence is not yet reached.
keyQuestions: What coverage gaps exist across SKILL.md, references, and assets?
What friction do users or agents encounter when routing, loading, applying, or verifying these skills?
Which upgrade proposals have the highest leverage and clearest evidence?
answeredQuestions: Ancillary README/checklist docs do not overturn the iteration 5 ranking; they strengthen proposal 1 and broaden proposal 3.
Stale path normalization must cover code-verify and code-debug READMEs/checklist assets, not just primary SKILL.md contracts.
ruledOut: Re-ranking stale-path cleanup above the broken verifier
Treating sibling links as completely broken
Editing target README/checklist files during research

focus: Implement and quality ancillary-doc convergence pass against ranked sk-code workflow proposals
noveltyJustification: 1 of 5 findings was fully new and 1 was partially new, while 3 findings were corroborative/redundant; no simplicity bonus was applied because convergence threshold is not yet reached.
keyQuestions: What coverage gaps exist across SKILL.md, references, and assets?
What friction do users or agents encounter when routing, loading, applying, or verifying these skills?
Which upgrade proposals have the highest leverage and clearest evidence?
answeredQuestions: Implement/quality ancillary docs corroborate the existing ranking rather than adding a new top proposal.
Path normalization should include code-implement/README.md and code-quality/README.md; the handoff schema should include advisory dist-warning semantics.
ruledOut: Promoting a new separate proposal for check-dist-staleness.sh
Merging code-quality and code-review
Adding local references directories under code-implement

focus: Final convergence synthesis for ranked sk-code workflow sub-skill upgrade proposals
noveltyJustification: 0 of 5 findings were new; this iteration synthesized stable prior evidence and found no ranking-changing contradiction, so the ratio is 0.00 and convergence is reached.
keyQuestions: Which upgrade proposals have the highest leverage and clearest evidence?
What friction do users or agents encounter when routing, loading, applying, or verifying these skills?
Where do code-implement, code-quality, code-debug, and code-verify overlap or contradict each other?
answeredQuestions: Legal convergence is reached for ranking: the top five proposals are stable across the last three passes and no new source class changed the order.
Remaining uncertainty is implementation detail, not research direction; exact patch shape and validation commands belong to a separate implementation-planning workflow.
ruledOut: New broad inventory pass
Re-ranking stale path cleanup above the broken verifier
Collapsing code-quality, code-verify, or code-review into one packet
